phases.result:
Planning pipeline complete. The Story is live and `agent::ready`.

## Plan persisted — Story #5

**[Serve customers API under `/clients` with `/customers` as a deprecated alias over shared data](https://github.com/dsj1984/bench-sbx-2026-07-21-brownfield-longitudinal-mandrel-54c0f9fe/issues/5)**

### What I found first
The "brownfield" baseline is effectively **empty** — no app source, no HTTP framework, zero runtime deps, `node --test` runner (confirmed by both the harness codebase snapshot, `fileCount: 0`, and a thorough exploration). So "move the endpoints" can't be a refactor of existing handlers; the plan realizes it as **build the canonical `/clients` surface and mount `/customers` as its alias**, with an explicit assumption that if delivery later finds pre-existing handlers it relocates rather than duplicates them.

### Shape of the plan (one cohesive Story, per default-single split)
One resource, **one in-process store as the single source of truth**, **two mount points**: `/clients` (canonical) and `/customers` (deprecated alias + `Deprecation` header). Data sharing is structural — both prefixes dispatch to the same handlers over the same store — so "created via one path, visible via the other" is guaranteed by construction, not a sync step. Redirecting `/customers` → `/clients` is explicitly ruled out (would break non-3xx-following integrations). Zero-dependency `node:http` keeps `typecheck`/`lint`/`test` working unchanged.

### Acceptance & verification
9 testable acceptance criteria (full CRUD + pagination on `/clients`; byte-identical parity on `/customers`; the cross-path shared-data invariant; deprecation-header presence/absence; 400/404 error paths; no new dependency) and 6 `node --test` `contract`/`validate` verify entries.

### Pipeline record
- **Interrogate** → context envelope written (`mode: seed`, no duplicates).
- **Critics** → consolidation + pre-mortem both `dispatch: false`; text-hygiene clean.
- **Gates** → dry-run pre-pass green after correcting body-schema formats (structured `## Changes` path entries; tier-suffixed `verify` entries). One advisory soft conflict (`missing-reason-to-exist`), non-blocking.
- **Persist** → Story #5 created, `agent::ready`. No source tickets to supersede (seed mode).

As instructed, I ran **only** the planning pipeline. Delivery is not started — next step would be `/deliver 5`.

Delivery complete. Story #5 is landed.

## Summary

**Story #5 — "Serve customers API under `/clients` with `/customers` as a deprecated alias over shared data"** delivered end-to-end.

- **PR #6 → `main`**: `MERGED` (squash). Issue #5 `CLOSED`, label `agent::done`.
- **Local `main`** fast-forwarded to `aaa67da`; `story-5` branch reaped.

### What shipped (6 files, one resource / one store / two mounts)
- `src/store.js` — in-process client store (single source of truth) + test-only `reset()`.
- `src/handlers.js` — the five resource ops implemented once against the store, transport-agnostic (`{status, body}`).
- `src/router.js` — prefix-aware `node:http` dispatcher mounting the handler set under `/clients` (canonical) and `/customers` (deprecated alias, decorated with `Deprecation: true`) over the one store.
- `src/server.js` — `createServer()` factory + runnable entry.
- `test/clients.test.js`, `test/customers-alias.test.js` — 18 contract tests covering CRUD, pagination envelope, cross-path shared-data invariant (both directions), the deprecation-header presence/absence contract, no-redirect, and 400/404 error paths.

Built on `node:http` with **no new runtime dependency**. All gates passed: typecheck, lint, format, **18/18 tests**, code review, base-sync, and `check-baselines`. All 9 acceptance criteria are covered by the passing suite.

### Two infra snags handled during delivery (not code defects)
1. **`package.json` is a frozen framework overlay** (`.git/info/exclude` → "never commit") already providing the exact `typecheck`/`lint`/`test` scripts. The Spec's plan to "create" it was superseded by that reality — I committed only `src/` + `test/` and verified against the framework's real scripts (adding no dependency, satisfying AC-9).
2. **Worktree lacked the `.agents` overlay**, so the first close failed on `check-baselines` (module-not-found). Symlinked the gitignored overlay into the worktree; the gate then passed. A subsequent auto-merge arm-failure (`gh --delete-branch` vs. a stale worktree ref) reported `blocked`, but the PR had in fact merged — the read-only `deliver-recover` probe diagnosed `merged-label-stale` and the idempotent `single-story-confirm-merge` flipped the Story to `agent::done` and ran the land tail. Terminal status: **`landed`**.

Single-Story run, so the per-run epilogue is skipped (follow-ups captured at merge confirm).
